question.text: According to DPIIT guidelines, a start-up in India must not have recorded a turnover of more than:

answer.text: INR 25 crore in any financial year
INR 50 crore in any financial year
INR 100 crore in any financial year
INR 200 crore in any financial year

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Government of India under DPIIT defines a start-up as a business that is not more than 10 years old and has not recorded a turnover of more than INR 100 crore in any financial year. The company must be engaged in innovation, development or improvement of products, or a scalable business model. **The Trap:** The INR 25 crore limit (Option A) was an earlier threshold that was subsequently raised. Candidates often confuse this with other thresholds like the angel fund investment limit of INR 25 crore. **Cross-Exam Context:** This definition is relevant for XIX-A and XIX-B exams and also appears in discussions about start-up ecosystem in Series X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Verify the DPIIT start-up status of investee companies before presenting VCF investments to clients. The DPIIT recognition provides tax benefits under Section 80-IAC and exemption from angel tax, which enhances the investment proposition.
